title: Storage, Filesystems, Archives, and Backups · Mastery Check
summary: Respond to low disk space and protect service data without confusing filesystems, mounts, archives, snapshots, and verified backups. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a storage map and restore-tested backup plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.
Distinguish apparent size, allocated blocks, free space, inodes, deleted-open files, and per-directory usage.
Use lsblk, findmnt, mount, and filesystem identifiers to observe topology before any mount or format action.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Block Filesystem Mount evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Block Filesystem Mount assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Filesystem / is 92% full with 300 free inodes while a deleted-open log consumes 1.4 GiB. Build an archive and restore proof without formatting, mounting, or deleting host data. Competency probe: Relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Block Filesystem Mount has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Block Filesystem Mount precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Filesystem / is 92% full with 300 free inodes while a deleted-open log consumes 1.4 GiB. Build an archive and restore proof without formatting, mounting, or deleting host data. Competency probe: Relate block devices, partitions, filesystems, mount points, namespaces, and directory visibility.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A mounted device becomes a new drive letter independent of the directory tree.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior for the quiz Block Filesystem Mount case
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary for the quiz Block Filesystem Mount case
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output for the quiz Block Filesystem Mount case
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff for the quiz Block Filesystem Mount case

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
